title: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE
display_title: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE
wikitext:
[]: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by Pathetic. » Wed Apr 03, 2019 3:21 am I came across someone who said they believed the rebuilds are a continuation of the main series. I asked them to explain it to me but the explanation was more about passive-aggressively insulting me than the actual theory. I was wondering if anyone could explain the theory to me, or give me a link to a page explaining it. Thanks in advance.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Apr 03, 2019 5:58 am You need to search for "Sequel Theory" and the related "[Time]Loop Theory". Here are a couple of threads from this forum to get you started: https://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6022/A-remake-or-a-sequel/ https://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13212/Sequel-theory-once-again/ But I think you'll find sequel theory is well out of favour with those who have looked at the films in more than a cursory manner.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by eldomtom2 » Fri Apr 05, 2019 1:59 am A sequel in the traditional sense, probably not. But the titles of the final Rebuild film all but confirm some sort of connection to the original series.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by Pathetic. » Fri Apr 05, 2019 2:25 am Thank you!

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Apr 05, 2019 6:12 am eldomtom2 wrote: A sequel in the traditional sense, probably not. But the titles of the final Rebuild film all but confirm some sort of connection to the original series.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by eldomtom2 » Fri Apr 05, 2019 12:27 pm The original series spells "Evangelion" as "エヴァンゲリオン". The Rebuilds spell it as "ヱヴァンゲリヲン", but with 3.0+1.0 it's back to "エヴァンゲリオン". The title has also gone from "Evangelion New Theatrical Version" to "Shin (specifically not the kanji for "new") Evangelion Theatrical Version". And if it was just a straightforward continuation from 3.33, why not call it 4.0? There is undoubtedly something going on.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Apr 05, 2019 2:53 pm 4 is an unlucky number in that region, I believe (like 13 in many other areas).

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by eldomtom2 » Fri Apr 05, 2019 5:26 pm I somehow doubt it's enough of an unlucky number to not use it in the title of a numerical sequel. And that's ignoring the rest of the evidence.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Apr 05, 2019 6:25 pm pwhodges wrote: 4 is an unlucky number in that region, I believe (like 13 in many other areas).

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 06, 2019 7:07 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: the current "old" Evangelion

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Apr 07, 2019 7:53 am ^I could see that. Do any of the other Eva properties or spin-offs besides NME use the Wevangeliwon spelling? 3.0's ending provided a nice set up to start over, just further in the future. Eva is a story that repeats. :P

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by JohnRaney » Wed Jun 05, 2019 3:29 pm Realize I'm late to this, but... I explain my thoughts on this quite a bit here: thread/19811/Evangelions-5-part-Structure/ I think the main Eva merchandise are all sequels, but less in a direct "Eva 2" kind of way and more in an abstract, fractured sense, which is perfectly in keeping with the series' post-modern and mystical ideas on the subjective nature of truth. Each one is a different view on the same story, revealing different aspects, but each one builds on previous iterations both literally and thematically.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by Keiji » Thu Jun 13, 2019 8:12 pm JohnRaney wrote: Realize I'm late to this, but... I explain my thoughts on this quite a bit here: thread/19811/Evangelions-5-part-Structure/ I think the main Eva merchandise are all sequels, but less in a direct "Eva 2" kind of way and more in an abstract, fractured sense, which is perfectly in keeping with the series' post-modern and mystical ideas on the subjective nature of truth. Each one is a different view on the same story, revealing different aspects, but each one builds on previous iterations both literally and thematically.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by JohnRaney » Tue Jul 02, 2019 7:10 am Thank you! And your English is fine.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by cathpeti47 » Thu Aug 08, 2019 8:14 pm eldomtom2 wrote: The original series spells "Evangelion" as "エヴァンゲリオン". The Rebuilds spell it as "ヱヴァンゲリヲン", but with 3.0+1.0 it's back to "エヴァンゲリオン". The title has also gone from "Evangelion New Theatrical Version" assurance décès to "Shin (specifically not the kanji for "new") Evangelion Theatrical Version". And if it was just a straightforward continuation from 3.33, why not call it 4.0? There is undoubtedly something going on.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Aug 09, 2019 2:28 pm Maybe it's as simple as 3.0 + 1.0 = 4.0.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by Melkor » Fri Aug 09, 2019 4:09 pm I always assumed that the reason it was called 3.0 + 1.0 instead of 4.0 was because of how the number four is considered to be very unlucky in Japan and a lot of other East Asian cultures, due to how similar it sounds to the word for death. Tetraphobia is so extreme in Japan that people will even go out of their way to avoid having phone numbers and addresses with the number four in them, and buildings would often skip the fourth floor, similar to how in the West buildings would skip the thirteenth floor. When it comes to fiction, authors and directors will go to some creative lengths to avoid calling something the fourth installment of a series. Calling it Shin Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0 could just be a fancy way of avoiding using the number four in the title.

[]: Re: Rebuilds being a continuation of EoE?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 10, 2019 2:24 am Don't we have a topic specifically for the number stuff? thread/16702/The-Eva-3010-Title/
